scope: Publicly described roles, departments, facilities and operating patterns from major UK multidisciplinary veterinary referral hospitals.

governance.permissionRule: Role templates describe work and accountability but never grant LucyWorks permissions automatically.
governance.verificationRule: A hospital must verify its own organisation chart, registrations, competencies, employment status and delegated authority before configuration is activated.
governance.freshnessRule: Recheck source pages before hospital adoption because staff, services and facilities change.

hospitals.name: Bristol Vet Specialists
publicOperatingModel: More than 100 professionals
Specialists supported by residents, interns, registered veterinary nurses, patient care assistants and support teams
24/7 hospital with out-of-hours urgent contact
Urgent referrals by telephone and non-urgent referrals by structured form with clinical history and test or image uploads
Separate feline and canine pathways
facilities: Ultrasound and specialist imaging reading capability
Separate dog and cat waiting, consulting and inpatient areas

hospitals.name: DWR Veterinary Specialists
hospitals.hospitalType: private multidisciplinary referral hospital and diagnostics laboratory
publicOperatingModel: Approximately 400 colleagues
Formal leadership, non-clinical management, nursing leadership, case management and clinical training structures
Dedicated case management and client care from referral contact through discharge
Service-based wards and dedicated night nursing model
RCVS Practice Standards Scheme participation
facilities: Service-specific medical, surgical, neurology, ophthalmology, feline and ICU wards
Integrated imaging with MRI and CT

hospitals.name: Willows Veterinary Specialists
publicOperatingModel: Specialist-led services with heads of service
24/7 emergency and inpatient care
Referral nursing shadowing and continuing professional development
Dedicated physiotherapy and rehabilitation
Clinical nutrition integrated into multidisciplinary care
facilities: Oxygen delivery, incubators and multi-parameter monitoring

hospitals.name: Southfields Veterinary Specialists
publicOperatingModel: Purpose-built 42,000 square foot hospital
Specialists, clinicians, residents, interns, nurses, patient care assistants, theatre technicians and support teams
24/7 emergency, inpatient and night nursing
Training placements, internships and residencies
Dedicated client care service
facilities: Approximately 195 kennels with separate feline provision
Dedicated emergency entrance and treatment centre
MRI, CT, two X-ray rooms, fluoroscopy and two ultrasound rooms
Linear accelerator and dedicated oncology suite
Two chemotherapy rooms and oncology ward

hospitals.name: RVC Queen Mother Hospital for Animals
hospitals.hospitalType: university teaching and multidisciplinary referral hospital
publicOperatingModel: 24/7/365 referral and emergency operation
Specialists, residents, interns, registered nurses, veterinary students and nursing students
Formal hospital coordination for patient throughput, procedures, anaesthesia and kennel occupancy
Teaching, clinical research and specialist training integrated with patient care
Level 1 veterinary trauma centre and blood donor programme
facilities: Blood donor programme and blood bank
Twenty-two-patient ICU described by the ECC training programme
Oxygen kennels, mechanical ventilators and in-house diagnostic analysers

roleTemplates.title: Clinical Director
roleTemplates.explanation: The senior clinician accountable for clinical standards, cross-specialty governance, clinical escalation and the safe design of services.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Hospital-defined senior clinical authority; never inferred from title alone.

roleTemplates.title: Deputy Clinical Director
roleTemplates.explanation: A delegated senior clinical leader who supports the Clinical Director and can hold defined organisation-wide clinical responsibilities.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Limited to explicit delegation recorded by the hospital.

roleTemplates.title: Hospital Director
roleTemplates.explanation: The senior executive accountable for the hospital as an operating organisation, including strategy, resources, performance and major risk.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Executive authority must be configured and separated from clinical decision authority.

roleTemplates.title: Hospital Manager
roleTemplates.explanation: The operational leader responsible for day-to-day hospital delivery, workforce coordination, service support and resolving non-clinical constraints.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Operational authority only unless a separate clinical delegation exists.

roleTemplates.title: Facilities Manager
roleTemplates.explanation: The role accountable for buildings, utilities, maintenance, access, equipment environments and facilities-related safety or downtime.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: May remove a facility from use; cannot independently declare clinical suitability.

roleTemplates.title: Head of Service or Department
roleTemplates.explanation: The accountable leader for a clinical discipline, responsible for service standards, staffing, competence, capacity and specialty escalation.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Authority is bounded to the configured service and professional scope.

roleTemplates.title: Head of Nursing Services
roleTemplates.explanation: The senior nursing leader accountable for nursing standards, workforce, professional development and patient-care delivery across the hospital.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Nursing professional authority within the hospital's governance model.

roleTemplates.title: Head Medical Services Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: A senior nurse leading inpatient, medicine and critical-care nursing services and coordinating standards across medical pathways.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Nursing authority for configured medical services.

roleTemplates.title: Head Surgical Services Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: A senior nurse leading theatre, peri-operative and surgical nursing operations, including readiness, staffing and standards.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Nursing and operational authority for configured surgical services.

roleTemplates.title: Head Support Services Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: A senior nursing role coordinating support functions that enable clinical teams, such as patient care assistants, stock, infection-control support or cross-service nursing resources.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Bounded to defined support services.

roleTemplates.title: Hospital Flow Coordinator
roleTemplates.explanation: A real-time coordination role that follows patients through admission, imaging, procedures, anaesthesia, wards and discharge while balancing room and kennel capacity.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Coordinates the plan but cannot replace clinical approval or professional judgement.

roleTemplates.title: Referral Coordinator
roleTemplates.explanation: The role that receives and checks referral information, coordinates with referring practices and services, and moves a referral toward acceptance and booking.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: May coordinate acceptance but clinical acceptance remains with an authorised clinician where required.

roleTemplates.title: Case Manager
roleTemplates.explanation: A continuity role ensuring the owner, referring practice and hospital teams receive coordinated information from first contact through safe discharge.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Coordinates information and process; does not make clinical decisions.

roleTemplates.title: Veterinary Specialist or Consultant
roleTemplates.explanation: A veterinary surgeon with advanced accredited training in a discipline who assesses complex referrals, determines diagnostic and treatment options, and remains accountable for specialist clinical decisions.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Clinical authority only within verified registration, specialty, competence and hospital privileges.

roleTemplates.title: Clinical Fellow
roleTemplates.explanation: An experienced clinician in a structured advanced clinical or academic post, usually working with senior specialist oversight and defined responsibilities.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Determined by individual privileges and supervision arrangements.

roleTemplates.title: Resident
roleTemplates.explanation: A veterinary surgeon undertaking several years of discipline-specific specialist training under formal supervision, with increasing but bounded case responsibility.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Requires explicit supervision and privilege rules; residency status is not independent specialist authority.

roleTemplates.title: Rotating Intern
roleTemplates.explanation: A qualified veterinary surgeon completing a supervised rotation across several referral disciplines and often supporting out-of-hours inpatient or emergency care.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Supervised and explicitly limited by the hospital.

roleTemplates.title: Service or Specialist Intern
roleTemplates.explanation: A qualified vet in a supervised internship focused on one discipline, positioned between broad rotating experience and residency-level training.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Limited by named supervisor and competency record.

roleTemplates.title: Registered Veterinary Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: A regulated nursing professional responsible for comprehensive patient care, nursing interventions, monitoring, anaesthesia support, diagnostics and surgical support within professional scope.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Professional nursing judgement within verified registration, competence and delegation.

roleTemplates.title: Senior or Lead Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: An experienced nurse leading a ward, shift or specialty team, coordinating workload and acting as the first nursing escalation point.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Configured shift or service leadership, not automatic hospital-wide authority.

roleTemplates.title: Specialty Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: An RVN with advanced experience in a particular service such as medicine, neurology, oncology, dermatology, cardiology or surgery.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Defined by registration, competency and local delegation.

roleTemplates.title: ICU or Emergency and Critical Care Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: A nurse focused on unstable or critically ill patients requiring intensive monitoring, rapid intervention, advanced lines, ventilation support and one-to-one care.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Critical nursing authority within protocol and escalation rules.

roleTemplates.title: Night Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: A nurse dedicated to overnight inpatient continuity, often assigned by ward and responsible for detecting change, completing care plans and escalating to the out-of-hours veterinary team.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Within nursing scope and night escalation policy.

roleTemplates.title: Student Veterinary Nurse
roleTemplates.explanation: A learner enrolled in formal veterinary nursing training who performs defined tasks under supervision while gathering competency evidence.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: No unsupervised authority beyond explicitly permitted training scope.

roleTemplates.title: Patient Care Assistant
roleTemplates.explanation: A clinical support colleague providing daily patient needs, comfort, enrichment, hygiene, procedure assistance and environmental infection-control support under direction.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: No independent clinical decision authority.

roleTemplates.title: Theatre Technician
roleTemplates.explanation: A technical role supporting operating-theatre preparation, equipment, consumables, cleaning, turnaround and procedural logistics.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Technical readiness authority; no independent clinical authority.

roleTemplates.title: Diagnostic Radiographer
roleTemplates.explanation: An imaging professional operating modalities such as CT and MRI and working with veterinary radiologists to acquire diagnostic-quality studies safely and efficiently.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Imaging technical authority within professional competence; diagnostic interpretation remains with authorised clinicians.

roleTemplates.title: Therapeutic Radiographer
roleTemplates.explanation: A radiotherapy professional supporting treatment planning, patient setup and safe delivery of radiation treatments using a linear accelerator.
typicalResponsibilities: coordination with radiation oncologists and anaesthesia
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Technical treatment-delivery authority under an approved clinical treatment plan.

roleTemplates.title: Clinical Pathologist
roleTemplates.explanation: A veterinary specialist who interprets laboratory findings from blood, urine, cytology and related diagnostic material and advises clinical teams.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Diagnostic interpretation within verified pathology specialty scope.

roleTemplates.title: Anatomic Pathologist
roleTemplates.explanation: A veterinary specialist interpreting tissue and post-mortem samples to diagnose disease and provide pathology reports.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Diagnostic interpretation within verified pathology specialty scope.

roleTemplates.title: Biomedical Scientist
roleTemplates.explanation: A laboratory professional performing and validating diagnostic testing under laboratory quality systems.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Laboratory technical authority according to the laboratory quality system.

roleTemplates.title: Laboratory Technician or Laboratory PCA
roleTemplates.explanation: A support role handling sample reception, preparation, equipment, consumables and routine laboratory processes under supervision.
lucyWorksCapabilities: flag missing identifiers or unsuitable samples
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: No independent specialist interpretation authority.

roleTemplates.title: Veterinary Physiotherapist or Rehabilitation Lead
roleTemplates.explanation: A rehabilitation professional assessing mobility and delivering physiotherapy, hydrotherapy and recovery plans alongside surgical, neurological and pain teams.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Within verified rehabilitation qualification and referral plan.

roleTemplates.title: Clinical Nutritionist
roleTemplates.explanation: A veterinary specialist providing nutritional assessment and treatment, including complex enteral or parenteral support integrated with other services.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Clinical authority within verified nutrition specialty scope.

roleTemplates.title: Client Care Associate or Receptionist
roleTemplates.explanation: The front-of-house and telephone role supporting owners and referring practices, handling arrival, enquiries, appointments and communication routing.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Administrative authority only.

roleTemplates.title: Insurance Administrator
roleTemplates.explanation: A specialist administrator handling direct and indirect claims, policy evidence, claim forms, pre-authorisation and communication about insurer decisions or shortfalls.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Administrative and financial-process authority; cannot promise insurer cover or alter clinical necessity.

roleTemplates.title: Administrator or Finance Administrator
roleTemplates.explanation: A hospital support role maintaining records, correspondence, finance processes, documents and organisational coordination.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Administrative authority only.

roleTemplates.title: Complaints Manager
roleTemplates.explanation: The accountable role receiving formal complaints, acknowledging them, coordinating investigation, keeping the complainant updated and recording learning actions.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Governance authority for the complaint process, not automatic clinical adjudication.

roleTemplates.title: Education or Training Programme Lead
roleTemplates.explanation: A role coordinating internships, residencies, placements, supervision, teaching objectives and assessment evidence.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: Education authority; clinical privileges remain separately governed.

roleTemplates.title: Final-Year Veterinary Student or Clinical Placement Student
roleTemplates.explanation: A veterinary student gaining supervised clinical experience and participating in defined patient-care activities without independent authority.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: No independent clinical authority.

roleTemplates.title: Veterinary Nursing Student or Placement Learner
roleTemplates.explanation: A nursing learner undertaking supervised clinical placements and competency development in patient care and client support.
roleTemplates.decisionAuthority: No independent authority outside explicitly permitted supervised activity.

departmentTemplates.name: Anaesthesia and Analgesia
departmentTemplates.purpose: Peri-operative assessment, anaesthetic planning, pain management, procedural sedation and support for critically ill patients.

departmentTemplates.name: Cardiology and Interventional Cardiology
departmentTemplates.purpose: Diagnosis and medical or interventional treatment of cardiac disease, including echocardiography and catheter-based procedures.

departmentTemplates.name: Dentistry, Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
departmentTemplates.purpose: Complex dental, oral, jaw and facial diagnosis and surgery.

departmentTemplates.name: Dermatology
departmentTemplates.purpose: Diagnosis and management of skin, ear, claw, allergic, infectious and immune-mediated disease.

departmentTemplates.name: Diagnostic Imaging
departmentTemplates.purpose: Acquisition and interpretation of radiography, ultrasound, CT, MRI and fluoroscopy studies.

departmentTemplates.name: Emergency and Critical Care / ICU
departmentTemplates.purpose: Emergency triage, stabilisation, intensive monitoring, ventilation and multidisciplinary management of critically ill patients.

departmentTemplates.name: Internal Medicine
departmentTemplates.purpose: Complex medical diagnosis and non-surgical treatment across gastroenterology, endocrinology, respiratory, renal, haematology, immune and infectious disease.

departmentTemplates.name: Interventional Radiology
departmentTemplates.purpose: Image-guided minimally invasive procedures such as stents, vascular occlusion and urinary bypass interventions.

departmentTemplates.name: Neurology and Neurosurgery
departmentTemplates.purpose: Diagnosis and medical or surgical management of brain, spinal cord, nerve and neuromuscular disease.

departmentTemplates.name: Medical Oncology and Radiotherapy
departmentTemplates.purpose: Cancer diagnosis and treatment through chemotherapy, surgery, radiotherapy, immunotherapy and palliative care.

departmentTemplates.name: Ophthalmology
departmentTemplates.purpose: Medical and surgical treatment of eye and vision disorders.

departmentTemplates.name: Orthopaedics
departmentTemplates.purpose: Diagnosis and surgical treatment of bones, joints, fractures, deformities and mobility disorders.

departmentTemplates.name: Soft Tissue Surgery
departmentTemplates.purpose: Complex surgery involving thoracic, abdominal, oncological, reconstructive and minimally invasive procedures.

departmentTemplates.name: Exotics
departmentTemplates.purpose: Specialist care for birds, reptiles, small mammals and other non-traditional companion species.

departmentTemplates.name: Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation
departmentTemplates.purpose: Mobility assessment, post-operative rehabilitation, neurological rehabilitation, chronic pain support and hydrotherapy.

departmentTemplates.name: Clinical Nutrition
departmentTemplates.purpose: Specialist nutritional assessment and enteral or parenteral support integrated with medical and surgical care.

departmentTemplates.name: Pathology and Diagnostic Laboratory
departmentTemplates.purpose: Sample reception, testing, quality control, clinical pathology, anatomic pathology and specialist interpretation.

departmentTemplates.name: Transfusion Medicine and Blood Bank
departmentTemplates.purpose: Blood donor management, product preparation, compatibility and transfusion support.

departmentTemplates.name: Behaviour
departmentTemplates.purpose: Assessment and management of behavioural problems and support for low-stress hospital design and handling.

departmentTemplates.name: Hospital Coordination and Flow
departmentTemplates.purpose: Real-time control of patient throughput, procedures, anaesthesia, rooms and inpatient capacity.

departmentTemplates.name: Referral and Case Management
departmentTemplates.purpose: Referral intake, information completeness, communication, booking and continuity from first contact to discharge.

departmentTemplates.name: Client Care and Reception
departmentTemplates.purpose: Arrival, enquiries, owner support, contact routing and front-of-house coordination.

departmentTemplates.name: Insurance and Finance
departmentTemplates.purpose: Estimates, payment status, direct and indirect claims, pre-authorisation and financial communication.

departmentTemplates.name: Education, Training and Research
departmentTemplates.purpose: Internships, residencies, student placements, supervision, competency assessment, teaching and clinical research.

departmentTemplates.name: Facilities and Estates
departmentTemplates.purpose: Building, utilities, access, maintenance, equipment environments, contractor control and facilities compliance.

workflowPatterns.name: Urgent and routine referral split
workflowPatterns.explanation: Urgent referrals use immediate telephone contact, while routine referrals use a structured submission with history, results and images.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Separate urgent intake from routine intake, require attachments, timestamp acceptance and preserve the referral source.

workflowPatterns.name: Referral completeness checking
workflowPatterns.explanation: Clinical history and relevant blood, urine, faecal, radiographic and ultrasound information are expected before booking or specialist review.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Use mandatory referral fields, attachment checks and a missing-information queue rather than free-text intake alone.

workflowPatterns.name: Consultation, options, estimate and owner decision
workflowPatterns.explanation: A specialist examines the patient, discusses possible causes and treatment options, provides an estimate and supports the owner to choose a plan.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Version options, estimate, consent and owner communication as linked evidence rather than a single mutable status.

workflowPatterns.name: Insurance claim and pre-authorisation workflow
workflowPatterns.explanation: Hospitals may manage direct or indirect claims, collect policy documents, request pre-authorisation and track insurer decisions or client shortfalls.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Insurance needs its own state machine and responsible administrator; insurer approval must never be represented as clinical approval.

workflowPatterns.name: Hospital flow coordination
workflowPatterns.explanation: A coordination team can track patients from admission to theatre and schedule diagnostic rooms, endoscopy, theatres, anaesthesia and ward capacity.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: The master grid must include patient, procedure, room, anaesthesia and inpatient-capacity dependencies in one operational plan.

workflowPatterns.name: Multidisciplinary case management
workflowPatterns.explanation: Complex patients are managed across specialties rather than transferred into isolated departmental silos.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Maintain one episode and one evidence trail with multiple consulting services, named primary accountability and explicit shared decisions.

workflowPatterns.name: Emergency triage, stabilisation and transfer
workflowPatterns.explanation: Emergency teams assess and stabilise first, then transfer the patient to ICU or the relevant specialty for ongoing management.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Support rapid emergency acceptance, stabilisation tasks, time-critical observations and controlled transfer of accountability.

workflowPatterns.name: Day and night inpatient continuity
workflowPatterns.explanation: Dedicated night nurses, PCAs and interns continue ward and ICU care after daytime services hand over.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Require named handover acceptance, ward assignment, overdue-care alerts and out-of-hours escalation routes.

workflowPatterns.name: Service-specific and species-specific warding
workflowPatterns.explanation: Hospitals separate patients by service, clinical risk and species, including feline areas and isolation facilities.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Capacity must be modelled by ward capability, species, infection risk and staffing rather than generic kennel count.

workflowPatterns.name: Case-management continuity
workflowPatterns.explanation: A dedicated team can oversee the owner and referring-vet journey from first contact to safe discharge.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Track communication duties, unresolved questions, discharge documents and referrer updates as owned work.

workflowPatterns.name: Supervised training pathway
workflowPatterns.explanation: Interns, residents and students work through defined rotations or specialty programmes with supervision and progressive responsibility.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Assignments must include supervisor, privilege level, training objective and required sign-off.

workflowPatterns.name: Formal complaints governance
workflowPatterns.explanation: A complaint is acknowledged, investigated by appropriate people, updated against a deadline and converted into learning actions.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Provide protected complaint cases, deadlines, investigation ownership, response evidence and improvement tracking.

workflowPatterns.name: Defined procedure package
workflowPatterns.explanation: Some hospitals publish packages combining consultation, imaging, anaesthesia, procedure, hospitalisation and specified exclusions.
workflowPatterns.lucyWorksImplication: Procedure catalogue entries should support included resources, limits, exclusions and versioned prices rather than one flat fee field.
